_meta.description: Federal student loan borrowing caps effective for periods of enrollment beginning on or after July 1, 2026, enacted by P.L. 119-21 §81001 (July 4, 2025; ED now styles the law the 'Working Families Tax Cuts Act', previously the 'One Big Beautiful Bill Act'), amending HEA §455(a) / 20 U.S.C. §1087e(a), implemented by ED's RISE final rule (91 FR 23768, May 1, 2026; FR Doc 2026-08556) amending 34 CFR 685.102/685.200/685.203. Drives /student-loan-cap-calculator/. STANDALONE dataset — student-aid (Title IV) policy, NOT tax; deliberately not co-located with obbba-deductions-2026.json and shares no engine with the tax/payroll cluster.
_meta.litigationFlag: The professional-degree definition (34 CFR 685.102) was preliminarily stayed by Judge Beryl Howell (D.D.C.) on June 24, 2026 (consolidated AANP v. McMahon / nursing-coalition + 25-state suits). During the stay, ED's interim guidance (FSA Electronic Announcement, June 29 2026, updated July 10 2026) recognizes 29 professional-degree programs — broader than the stayed rule's 11 fields. RE-CHECK THE FSA EA BEFORE EVERY DEPLOY of this tool.
_meta.confidence: HIGH on every dollar cap ($20,500 / $50,000 / $100,000 / $200,000 / $257,500 / $20,000 / $65,000 — statute + verbatim final-rule text); MEDIUM on the $224,000 health-professions legacy aggregate (from ED's NSLDS EA summary and CRS, not verbatim-quoted regulatory text — used only in a non-binding legacy note); the professional-vs-graduate classification itself is mid-litigation and can change.

effective: Periods of enrollment/instruction beginning on or after July 1, 2026 (20 U.S.C. §1087e(a)(3)-(6); 34 CFR 685.203)

aggregateExcludes: old Grad PLUS balances (those count only against the $257,500 lifetime cap)
graduate.note: Direct Unsubsidized only — subsidized loans for graduate students ended in 2012 and were not revived. The $100,000 applies to a graduate student who is not (and has not been) a professional student; otherwise the shared $200,000 pool applies (20 U.S.C. §1087e(a)(4)(B)).

professional.note: Graduate and professional borrowing share one $200,000 pool — borrowing in one status shrinks the other (20 U.S.C. §1087e(a)(4)(B)).

excludes: Parent PLUS borrowed on behalf of a dependent student
consolidation loan shell (the underlying loans are included)
lifetime.note: True odometer: 'without regard to any amounts repaid, forgiven, canceled, or otherwise discharged' (20 U.S.C. §1087e(a)(6); 34 CFR 685.203(j)(2)). Returned loan funds do not count.

parentPlus.note: NEW caps per dependent student, combined across ALL parents: $20,000/academic year, $65,000 aggregate 'without regard to any amounts repaid, forgiven, canceled, or otherwise discharged' (20 U.S.C. §1087e(a)(5); 34 CFR 685.203(f)(2), (g)(2)). Excluded from the $257,500 lifetime cap. Returned funds do not count.

undergraduate.note: P.L. 119-21 does not amend annual or aggregate limits for loans to undergraduate students (CRS R48727; 34 CFR 685.203(a)-(d) untouched). Only the parent (Parent PLUS) side changed.

legacyException.conditions: Enrolled in the program of study as of June 30, 2026 AND a Direct Loan was made for that program before July 1, 2026 (for the Parent PLUS exception: a Direct Loan to the parent borrower OR to the dependent student qualifies). Voided if the student withdraws / ceases enrollment; changing majors within the same degree preserves it (34 CFR 685.203(b)(2)(iv)(B)-(C), (f)(2)(ii)/(iv), (g)(3)/(5); 20 U.S.C. §1087e(a)(8)).
legacyException.durationRule: Expected time to credential = the lesser of 3 academic years (clock runs from July 1, 2026) or the remaining program length (34 CFR 685.102(b)); it can never run past ~June 30, 2029.
legacyException.effect: While it lasts, the new annual, aggregate, Parent PLUS, and $257,500 limits do NOT apply; Grad PLUS and Parent PLUS remain available up to cost of attendance minus other financial assistance; the pre-OBBBA unsubsidized aggregate applies instead.

litigation.status: Professional-degree definition preliminarily stayed in federal court; ED interim 29-program list in effect
litigation.stayCourt: U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia (Judge Beryl Howell)
litigation.note: Whether a program counts as professional ($50,000/yr, $200,000 pool) or graduate ($20,500/yr, $100,000 pool) is in active litigation and can change while the case proceeds. This tool does not classify your program — select the status your school's financial aid office confirms, and re-check ED's current list.

coaRule: No Direct Loan may exceed the cost of attendance minus other financial assistance (34 CFR 685.203(j)(1)); schools may also set lower, consistently-applied program-wide limits (34 CFR 685.203(m)(2)); less-than-full-time annual amounts are prorated by enrollment intensity, rounded to the nearest whole percentage point (20 U.S.C. §1087e(a)(7); 34 CFR 685.203(m)(1)).

sources.claim: All caps + interim exception, statutory text (20 U.S.C. §1087e(a), as amended by P.L. 119-21 §81001)
RISE final rule regulatory text + counting-method preamble (91 FR 23768, May 1, 2026)
NSLDS aggregate processing: lifetime-cap composition, per-student Parent PLUS, legacy aggregates (FSA EA Apr 24, 2026, updated May 7, 2026)
29-program professional-degree list during the court stay (FSA EA Jun 29, 2026, updated Jul 10, 2026)
